title: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes
display_title: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes
wikitext:
[]: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Angel » Sun Oct 09, 2022 3:23 pm SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Oct 10, 2022 3:04 pm If memory at all serves me, they're from the Anno at the elementary school interview thing; the bits that I do remember well are heavily misquoted to be much more "don't think about it, it means nothing" than the actual subs were

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Angel » Sun Oct 30, 2022 11:03 pm Blockio wrote: If memory at all serves me, they're from the Anno at the elementary school interview thing

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Nov 02, 2022 1:41 pm A link would be here . As far as Sadamoto goes; some time in the early 2000s, he has gone on record saying that he has not watched Eva beyond the single digit episodes and, judging by the things he blatantly gets wrong about the events of the show, it is fairly safe to say that years later he still hasn't. His involvement with the show was mechanical design, not writing; anything he has to say regarding the story and themes (especially the latter given his visible lack of understanding thereof again in Anima) is best taken with a massive grain of salt.

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Wed Nov 02, 2022 2:58 pm ^ You’re talking about Yamashita here, right?

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Thu Nov 03, 2022 12:38 pm Oh, right, got the names mixed up in my head, that one's on me.

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by AWinters » Wed Nov 09, 2022 6:21 am Blockio wrote: As far as Sadamoto goes; some time in the early 2000s, he has gone on record saying that he has not watched Eva beyond the single digit episodes and, judging by the things he blatantly gets wrong about the events of the show, it is fairly safe to say that years later he still hasn't.

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Nov 09, 2022 1:42 pm The only thing I ever recall reading to that tune is Sadamoto (yes, actually the right name this time ) saying that the final chapter with Mari in it was his own thing, has no bearing on NTE and hasn't been substantially talked over with Anno, so I assume that he had a fair bit of freedom

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Nov 14, 2022 12:31 pm It was "his thing", after all. But I bet Anno had at least an idea of what was going on with the manga. Frankly, he borrowed a lot of stuff from it and that's pretty obvious. Sadamoto did design Mari, if I'm not mistaken. So he probably knew a thing or two about her backstory and all (if that was even finalized when 2.0 came out, which I believe it was).

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Nov 14, 2022 3:50 pm I think it's the other way around; it's far more likelys that Sadamoto included things he thought were cool from the production of NTE, rather than Anno seeing the manga and incorporating that.

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Nov 17, 2022 10:39 am I think both things could have happened. Anno is not as detached from his work as some may say. He likes manga and anime, so he probably did take a look at what Sadamoto was writing. In the end of the day, it's his franchise. He most definitely didn't supervise it or anything, though. That's merely speculation, of course.

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Angel » Thu Dec 01, 2022 5:19 pm Blockio wrote: A link would be here .

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Dec 02, 2022 4:28 pm Not in the "nothing means anything stop thinking about it" way that the screenshot is trying to imply. Sure, the original decision was probably made because it was a cool sounding word, but going forward from that, there was a lot of legwork put into working the aesthetic into the themes and lore

[]: Re: Can anyone verify the authenticity of these quotes?             Quote Post by Angel » Sat Dec 03, 2022 9:55 pm I just meant "that's one source found, and all the rest still to go". We can worry about interpretation after the quotes have been verified.
